description: Tooling to keep the aim graph healthy as it accumulates cruft over long autonomous-development runs: duplicates, resolved-but-uncollapsed chains, status drift from the actual code, and vague catch-all parents. The reorganization of subtrees (e.g. the 'UI' catch-all, see 18263fc2) should become a byproduct of this tooling rather than a manual slog. Evidence motivating this: 2a9e3aba and f592b156 are byte-identical duplicates; a05ea545/891e2ee0/855e26a1 are three aims for one shipped feature; 8ae9e8da/fe0e0aaf/b3f8518f/4e50493a were all already implemented but still marked open.

status.comment: Status reconciled 2026-06-15 (the prior comment was stale — it listed merge-aim/hygiene-report/reparent-suggester as remaining, but all three are done). Built and done: duplicate finder (75af098f), merge_aims (338c12e5), graph_hygiene report (d6734796), reparent suggester (b0fb3242), and bge threshold recalibration (f82a961e, defaults 0.90/0.93→0.89). The maintenance tooling itself is essentially complete. Genuinely remaining: status-reconciliation (7625d5e6) is an ongoing/recurring pass, not a one-shot; taxonomy convention (c69ab6f7) is human-dependent — needs Felix to define the real structural parents (graph view / list view+vim / project loading / watchdog UI) before suggest_reparents can drive the 'Rearrange UI aims' (18263fc2) cleanup.
